evals.prompt: I want to add Scandit Label Capture to my web app. Here's my main file: empty-app.ts. The label is a perishable-product sticker with an EAN-13 barcode and an expiry date (required) and a total price (optional).
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, walks through the interactive label-definition questions, writes complete Label Capture integration code into empty-app.ts, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions @scandit/web-datacapture-core, @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode, and @scandit/web-datacapture-label npm packages
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with libraryLocation and moduleLoaders
labelCaptureLoader() is present in moduleLoaders
A license key placeholder string is present (semantic)
new LabelCaptureSettingsBuilder() is used with .addLabel(...).build()
new LabelDefinitionBuilder( is present
.build( is present
new CustomBarcodeBuilder() is used, with setSymbology(Symbology.EAN13UPCA) or setSymbologies including EAN13UPCA, and .build("Barcode") or a similar field name
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
new ExpiryDateTextBuilder() is used with .build("Expiry Date") or similar field name
new TotalPriceTextBuilder() is used with .build("Total Price") or similar field name, and is marked as optional (isOptional(true))
LabelCapture.forContext( is present
LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.withLabelCaptureForView( is present
addListener with didUpdateSession callback is present
session.capturedLabels is accessed inside didUpdateSession
Camera.pickBestGuess() is called
camera.applySettings(LabelCapture.createRecommendedCameraSettings()) is called
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called
No renamed-away regex properties: pattern, patterns, dataTypePattern, dataTypePatterns are absent from the generated code
No v8.5-only factory functions: labelCaptureSettings(, label(, customBarcode(, expiryDateText(, totalPriceText( are absent
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is NOT used (the user did not ask for Validation Flow)

evals.prompt: Add Label Capture to my empty-app.ts with the redesigned Validation Flow. The label is a perishable-product sticker with an EAN-13 barcode (required) and an expiry date (required). My package.json pins @scandit/web-datacapture-label to 8.2.0.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the SDK version from package.json and uses the redesigned Validation Flow API (no deprecated text properties, uses setPlaceholderTextForLabelDefinition).

assertions.text: A minimal integration with context, view, camera, settings, and mode is present (semantic)
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay.withLabelCaptureForView( is used instead of (or in addition to) LabelCaptureBasicOverlay
LabelCaptureValidationFlowSettings.create() is called
setPlaceholderTextForLabelDefinition is called on the validation-flow settings at least once
overlay.listener is assigned with onValidationFlowLabelCaptured and onManualInput handlers
Deprecated Validation Flow properties are absent: requiredFieldErrorText, missingFieldsHintText, manualInputButtonText do NOT appear in the generated code
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled

evals.prompt: Add Label Capture with the Validation Flow to my empty-app.ts. The label is a perishable-product sticker with an EAN-13 barcode (required) and an expiry date (required). My package.json pins @scandit/web-datacapture-label to 8.1.0.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects the v8.1 SDK version and uses the v8.1 Validation Flow API: async setter methods for text customisation and a listener with only onValidationFlowLabelCaptured (onManualInput was added in v8.2 and does not exist yet in v8.1).

assertions.text: LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay.withLabelCaptureForView( is used
LabelCaptureValidationFlowSettings.create() is called
At least one of await validationFlowSettings.setRequiredFieldErrorText, setMissingFieldsHintText, or setManualInputButtonText is called (v8.1 async setter API)
Text customisation is done via async method calls (setXxx(...)) not property assignments (xxx = ...)
setPlaceholderTextForLabelDefinition is NOT called (not available in v8.1)
overlay.listener is assigned with only onValidationFlowLabelCaptured — onManualInput is NOT in the listener object (it was added in v8.2)
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled

evals.prompt: I'm scanning a smart-device box label on web (empty-app.ts). It has two IMEI barcodes, a serial number barcode, and a part number barcode. Use the pre-built semantic barcode field types rather than plain custom barcodes so I get the tuned defaults.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the semantic pre-built barcode field builders ImeiOneBarcodeBuilder, ImeiTwoBarcodeBuilder, SerialNumberBarcodeBuilder, and PartNumberBarcodeBuilder (each awaited and built with .build(name)) instead of CustomBarcodeBuilder, and adds them to the LabelDefinitionBuilder with the matching addXxxBarcode methods.

assertions.text: new ImeiOneBarcodeBuilder() is used and added via .addImeiOneBarcode(...) for the first IMEI field
new ImeiTwoBarcodeBuilder() is used and added via .addImeiTwoBarcode(...) for the second IMEI field
new SerialNumberBarcodeBuilder() is used and added via .addSerialNumberBarcode(...) for the serial number field
new PartNumberBarcodeBuilder() is used and added via .addPartNumberBarcode(...) for the part number field
Each builder is awaited and built with .build("...") returning a Promise (e.g. await new ImeiOneBarcodeBuilder().build("IMEI 1"))
These pre-built barcode field builders are imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-label
CustomBarcodeBuilder is NOT used for the IMEI, serial number, or part number fields
new LabelCaptureSettingsBuilder() is used with .addLabel(...).build()

evals.prompt: I want to scan a retail price tag (price, unit price, weight) on web. Is there a ready-made label for this so I don't have to define each field myself? Write the setup into empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built LabelDefinition.createPriceCaptureDefinition(name) (awaited) instead of hand-defining fields, and warns that the PriceCapture pre-made definition is NOT compatible with the Validation Flow (so the Basic Overlay path must be used).

assertions.text: await LabelDefinition.createPriceCaptureDefinition( is used to create the price label definition
The pre-made definition is passed to new LabelCaptureSettingsBuilder().addLabel(...) without mutating it (no extra fields added to the returned definition)
The response warns that the PriceCapture pre-made definition is NOT compatible with the Validation Flow overlay and uses / recommends the LabelCaptureBasicOverlay path instead
LabelDefinition is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-label
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is NOT used with the pre-built price definition

evals.prompt: I'm using Smart Label Capture on web with the Basic Overlay (no Validation Flow). I want to highlight the barcode field in cyan and the expiry date field in orange in the camera preview, and react when the user taps a label. Show me how to customize the field brushes.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a LabelCaptureBasicOverlay listener implementing brushForField that returns a per-field Brush keyed off field.name, constructed with Brush and Color.fromRGBA from @scandit/web-datacapture-core, and implements onLabelTapped.

assertions.text: A LabelCaptureBasicOverlay is created (LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.withLabelCaptureForView) and its listener implements brushForField (called per field to decide the brush)
The brushForField implementation switches on field.name and returns a different Brush for the barcode field vs the expiry date field
Brush and Color are imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core and a new Brush(...) is constructed (or Brush.transparent is used)
The brush color uses transparency (alpha < 1, e.g. Color.fromRGBA(0, 255, 255, 0.5)) so the captured content is not occluded
onLabelTapped is implemented on the basic overlay listener to react to taps on a label
LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay is NOT used — the Basic Overlay path is used

evals.prompt: My customer's shipping labels are worn and the on-device OCR misses fields. Can I use a cloud fallback on web to improve accuracy? Add it to my existing label definition.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the Adaptive Recognition Engine by calling .adaptiveRecognitionMode(AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto) on the LabelDefinitionBuilder, flags that ARE is a Beta feature, notes it works only with the Validation Flow overlay, and that production use requires contacting Scandit Support (support@scandit.com).

assertions.text: AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto is applied to the label definition via .adaptiveRecognitionMode(AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto) on the LabelDefinitionBuilder
AdaptiveRecognitionMode is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-label
The response flags that the Adaptive Recognition Engine is a Beta feature that may change in future SDK versions
The response states that ARE works ONLY with the Validation Flow overlay (LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay), not the Basic or Advanced overlay
The response states that enabling ARE requires a license key with the ARE feature flag and that production use requires contacting Scandit Support at support@scandit.com

evals.prompt: I want to scan whole receipts on web and get back the store name, total, and line items. How do I set that up with Scandit?
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay with a listener whose onRecognized callback receives a ReceiptScanningResult (storeName, paymentTotal, lineItems, etc.), and flags that Receipt Scanning is a Beta feature built on the Adaptive Recognition Engine that requires Scandit Support (support@scandit.com) to enable.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay.withLabelCaptureForView( is used (the separate receipt-scanning overlay, NOT the standard Basic or Validation Flow overlay)
The overlay listener implements onRecognized and reads fields off the ReceiptScanningResult (e.g. result.storeName, result.paymentTotal, result.lineItems)
LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay (and ReceiptScanningResult) are imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-label
The response flags that Receipt Scanning is a Beta feature built on the Adaptive Recognition Engine and may change in future SDK versions
The response states that enabling it requires contacting Scandit Support at support@scandit.com
